title: Render Java Strategy Pattern (interface + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a Java Strategy pattern: an interface, one or more concrete strategy classes, and a context class that holds a swappable strategy. Uses the @@context_class@@ and @@package_name@@ placeholders.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy interface name).

parameters.name: package_name
parameters.description: Java package name, e.g. com.example.patterns.strategy.

title: Render TypeScript Strategy Pattern (interface + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a TypeScript Strategy pattern: an interface, one or more concrete strategy classes, and a context class. Includes a Vitest suite. Uses @@context_class@@ placeholder.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy interface name).

title: Render Python Strategy Pattern (Protocol/ABC + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a Python Strategy pattern using Protocol: a strategy protocol, one or more concrete strategy classes, a context class, and a pytest test module. Uses the @@context_class@@ placeholder.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy protocol name).

parameters.name: module_name
parameters.description: Python module base name (snake_case), e.g. order_strategy.

title: Render Python Function Stub from Signature
description: Renders a deterministic Python function stub (signature + docstring + NotImplementedError body) from a structured signature payload. Used by planning to seed executable code skeletons before the worker fills the body.

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: Snake_case function name to render.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: List of 'name:type' argument strings.

parameters.name: return_type
parameters.description: Return type annotation; default 'None'.

parameters.name: docstring
parameters.description: Docstring body (without triple quotes).

parameters.name: indent
parameters.description: Indentation width in spaces.

steps.name: validate_signature
steps.description: Ensure function_name matches python identifier rules and args list parses.

steps.name: render_body
steps.description: Emit indented docstring and 'raise NotImplementedError("...")' body.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Rendered source must parse via ast.parse without errors.
No imports are emitted — caller decides imports.

examples.name: simple_add
must_contain: def add(a: int, b: int) -> int:

title: Emit Sequential Workflow Definition
description: Emits a hub-recognizable workflow definition JSON for a sequence of N named steps with deterministic IDs. Language-agnostic: same shape works for Python/Java/TS tasks.

parameters.name: workflow_name
parameters.description: Workflow identifier (snake_case).

parameters.name: step_names
parameters.description: Ordered list of step display names.

parameters.name: depends_on_prev
parameters.description: If true, each step depends on the previous one (linear chain).

steps.name: build_steps
steps.description: For each name, mint a step_id 'step_<index>_<slugified_name>' and assign order index.

steps.name: wire_dependencies
steps.description: When depends_on_prev is true, each step's depends_on references the previous step_id.

steps.name: emit_workflow
steps.description: Serialize the workflow with name, version, and steps to a JSON object.

invariants: step_id is unique within the workflow.
No step depends on a forward step (no cycles).
Output JSON sorts keys lexicographically for stable diffs.

title: Generate Vitest Scaffold from Contract
description: Generates a vitest spec file skeleton (describe + it blocks, AAA comments, placeholder assertions) for a TypeScript contract or component name.

parameters.name: subject_name
parameters.description: Name of the function/class/component under test.

parameters.name: behavior_count
parameters.description: Number of 'it' blocks to scaffold.

parameters.name: imports
parameters.description: List of import strings to emit at the top.

steps.name: emit_imports
steps.description: Emit the provided import lines plus default vitest imports (describe, it, expect).

steps.name: scaffold_describe
steps.description: Emit a describe(subject_name, () => { ... }) block with behavior_count it() entries.

steps.name: insert_placeholders
steps.description: Each it() gets AAA comments and a single expect(true).toBe(true) placeholder.

invariants: Output is valid TypeScript that tsc --noEmit accepts when placeholders are replaced.
acceptance_gates: Output contains exactly one describe() call
Output imports describe, it, expect from 'vitest'

title: Wrap CLI Invocation with Retry and Log
description: Wraps an arbitrary CLI command in a retry loop with exponential backoff and structured logging. Language-agnostic — produces a shell script or a callable wrapper contract.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The CLI command (single string) to wrap.

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Number of retry attempts (>=1).

parameters.name: backoff_seconds
parameters.description: Initial backoff seconds; doubled per attempt.

parameters.name: log_file
parameters.description: Where to append the structured retry log lines.

steps.name: validate_command
steps.description: Reject empty command or command containing 'rm -rf /' (safety invariant).

steps.name: loop_with_backoff
steps.description: For attempt in 1..max_attempts: run command, on non-zero exit, sleep backoff * 2^(attempt-1), retry.

steps.name: log_outcome
steps.description: Append a single JSON line with timestamp, command, attempt, exit_code to log_file.

invariants: Refuses commands matching the deny-pattern list (rm -rf /, :(){:|:&};:, mkfs).
Logs are append-only — never truncates an existing log_file.
Total wall-clock time is bounded by max_attempts * sum(backoff_seconds * 2^i).
acceptance_gates: Empty command is rejected at validation step
Successful first attempt produces exactly one log line
Failed final attempt produces max_attempts log lines

title: Default-Deny Capability Gate
description: Java class that gates a capability behind an explicit allow-list. Default-deny: any capability not in the allow-list throws SecurityException. Used to enforce least-privilege in worker code.

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: Name of the gate class (PascalCase).

parameters.name: allowed_capabilities
parameters.description: List of capability strings that are explicitly allowed.

parameters.name: package_name
parameters.description: Java package for the generated class.

steps.name: emit_package_and_imports
steps.description: Emit 'package <name>;' and any required imports (java.util.Set, java.util.HashSet).

steps.name: emit_allow_set
steps.description: Generate a private static final Set<String> ALLOWED initialized from allowed_capabilities.

steps.name: emit_check_method
steps.description: Emit public static void require(String capability) that throws SecurityException when capability not in ALLOWED.

invariants: ALLOWED is populated at class-load time and never mutated.
require() throws SecurityException for any capability not in ALLOWED (default-deny).
Empty allowed_capabilities is permitted and means 'deny everything'.
